conditions: Insomnia related to newborn sleep disruption
health.mental_health: Increased anxiety and irritability since the baby arrived; feeling guilt and helplessness while supporting a partner with postpartum mood symptoms, plus fear of slipping at work. Uses therapy intermittently and relies on reminders/routines to manage ADHD under sleep deprivation.
recent_visits: Primary care visit (Jan) for sleep problems and headache frequency
Psychiatry telehealth follow-up (Feb) for ADHD medication management
Allergist appointment (Mar) to adjust seasonal allergy plan
Couples counseling intake (Feb) focused on postpartum adjustment

finance.debt: Owes about $22k in federal student loans; carrying ~$6k on a credit card after unpaid parental leave and unexpected baby-related costs; worried about making minimums if overtime tutoring dries up.
investments: 403(b) with a target-date index fund (small monthly contribution)
High-yield savings account for emergency fund (currently low balance)
recent_transactions: Hospital/OB-related bills and pediatric copays paid via card
Daycare deposit and registration fee for a center in North Raleigh
Purchased a second-hand bassinet and breast pump accessories
Paid for couples therapy copay and ADHD telehealth visit
Car repair (new tires) after a blowout on I-40

relationships: Spouse: Priya Reddy (currently struggling with postpartum depression/anxiety symptoms; Sanjay is primary support at home)
Infant son: Arjun (born recently; frequent nighttime wakeups)
Mother: Lakshmi Reddy (calls daily, offers advice that sometimes adds pressure)
Close friend/coworker: Megan Holloway (trusted confidant about work stress)
Neighborhood parent group contact: Jordan Fields (shares childcare tips and hand-me-downs)
social.social_media_activity: Mostly lurks on local parenting and Raleigh community groups; posts occasional baby photos with privacy settings tightened; avoids discussing mental health publicly but follows ADHD organization pages and teacher forums.
private_conversations: Discussing Priya’s postpartum symptoms, safety planning, and whether to seek medication
Admitting to his principal-level mentor that he’s struggling to keep up with grading and morning routines
Talking with a friend about fears of being judged as an inattentive parent due to ADHD
Asking other parents about affordable daycare and short-term babysitting options

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Fri 7:15am–3:30pm at school; typically stays until 4:30pm one or two days for planning/meetings; occasional evening tutoring 1–2 nights/week when feasible.
regular_appointments: Pediatrician check-ins and vaccine schedule (varies, typically mornings)
Couples therapy every other Wednesday at 7:30pm (telehealth)
upcoming_events: Parent-teacher conferences next month (two late evenings)
Daycare start date orientation in two weeks
Spring allergy peak—planned pharmacy pickup of nasal spray refill
Family visit from Priya’s sister for a weekend to help with the baby
schedule.daily_routine: Up around 5:30–6:00am depending on baby; quick breakfast and meds; school day with structured lesson blocks; after school planning/grading squeezed into short windows; evenings rotate feeding/soothing with spouse, prep bottles, and try to sleep in 2–3 hour stretches.

professional.company: Wake County Public School System (public elementary school in Raleigh)
projects: Implementing small-group reading interventions for below-grade-level students
Updating IEP/504 accommodation notes and progress monitoring
Preparing end-of-grade practice activities and spring benchmarking
Building a classroom behavior plan aligned with PBIS expectations
professional.performance_issues: Sleep deprivation and ADHD symptoms have led to missed minor deadlines (late data entry and delayed grading). He worries about being seen as unreliable during evaluation season and has asked for informal support from his grade-level chair rather than formal accommodations.
workplace_relationships: Grade-level chair (supportive but expects timely documentation)
Assistant principal (data-focused, has been pushing for faster progress monitoring)
School counselor (collaborates on student behavior plans; aware he’s under strain)

hobbies: Pick-up cricket in a local park when time allows
Cooking quick South Indian meals on weekends (batch cooking)
Podcasts/audiobooks about teaching strategies and ADHD coping
Short walks on the Neuse River Trail with stroller
habits: Uses a phone-based task manager with alarms to compensate for ADHD
Drinks 2–3 coffees most weekdays to stay alert (sometimes late afternoon, worsening sleep)
Occasionally uses melatonin to reset sleep after rough nights
Stress snacking on sweets during late-night feedings
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a two-bedroom apartment in Raleigh; nursery set up in the second bedroom; spouse currently on leave and he is balancing school demands with nighttime baby care. Considering moving closer to daycare but hesitant due to costs.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Mostly vegetarian at home with occasional chicken; tries to pack simple lunches (rice, dal, yogurt, fruit) but often ends up grabbing cafeteria snacks when overwhelmed; limits dairy during peak allergy season when congestion is bad.
